description: 1894–1895 war between the Qing dynasty and the Empire of Japan over influence in Joseon, fought chiefly in Joseon
description_wiki: The First Sino-Japanese War (25 July 1894 – 17 April 1895), or the First China–Japan War, was a conflict between the Qing dynasty of China and the Empire of Japan primarily for influence over Korea.

description: conflict between the Russian and Japanese empires from 1904 to 1905
description_wiki: The Russo-Japanese War (8 February 1904 – 5 September 1905) was fought between the Russian Empire and the Empire of Japan over rival imperial ambitions in Manchuria and the Korean Empire.

description: global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: dynamite explosion on railway line near Mukden (Shenyang) in 1931
earthquake
school of Buddhism in Japan
description_wiki: Shingon (真言宗, Shingon-shū; "True Word/Mantra School") is one of the major schools of Buddhism in Japan and one of the few surviving Vajrayana lineages in East Asian Buddhism.

description: branch of Buddhism based on the teachings of the 13th-century Japanese monk Nichiren
description_wiki: Nichiren Buddhism (Japanese: 日蓮仏教, romanized: Nichiren bukkyō), also known as Hokkeshū (Japanese: 法華宗, meaning Lotus Sect), is a branch of Mahayana Buddhism based on the teachings of the 13th-century Japanese Buddhist priest Nichiren (1222–1282) and is one of the Kamakura period schools.

description: school of Mahāyāna Buddhist Tradition
description_wiki: Zen (Japanese pronunciation: [dzeꜜɴ, dzeɴ]; from Chinese: Chan; in Korean: Sŏn, and Vietnamese: Thiền) is a Mahayana Buddhist tradition that developed in China during the Tang dynasty by blending Indian Mahayana Buddhism, particularly Yogacara and Madhyamaka philosophies, with Chinese Taoist thought, especially Neo-Daoist.

description: Japanese Buddhist monk and calligrapher, the founder of the Shingon school (774–835)
description_wiki: Kūkai (空海; Japanese pronunciation: [kɯꜜː.kai], 27 July 774 – 22 April 835), born Saeki no Mao (佐伯 眞魚) posthumously called Kōbō Daishi (弘法 大師; [koː.boː daꜜi.ɕi], lit. 'the Grand Master Who Propagated the Dharma'), was a Japanese Buddhist monk, calligrapher, and poet who founded the esoteric Shingon school of Buddhism.

description: fourth largest island of Japan
description_wiki: Shikoku (四国, Shikoku; pronounced [ɕi̥ꜜ.ko.kɯ, ɕi̥.koꜜ.kɯ] , lit. 'four provinces') is the smallest of the four main islands of Japan.

description: Abrahamic monotheistic religion founded on the teachings, person and life of Jesus
description_wiki: Christianity is an Abrahamic monotheistic religion, which states that Jesus is the Son of God and rose from the dead after his crucifixion, whose coming as the messiah (Christ) was prophesied in the Old Testament and chronicled in the New Testament.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: prefecture of Japan
description_wiki: Kōchi Prefecture (高知県, Kōchi-ken; Japanese pronunciation: [koꜜː.tɕi, koː.tɕi̥ꜜ.keɴ], locally [koː.tɕi]) is a prefecture of Japan located on the island of Shikoku.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: county seat of San Joaquin County, California, United States
description_wiki: Stockton is a city in and the county seat of San Joaquin County, California, United States.

description: capital city of the U.S. state of California and seat of Sacramento County
description_wiki: Sacramento is the capital city of the U.S. state of California.

description: county seat of Riverside County, California, United States
description_wiki: Riverside is a city in and the county seat of Riverside County, California, United States.

description: city in Alameda County, California, United States

description_wiki: Naples, California may refer to:

description: city in and county seat of Santa Barbara County, California, United States
description_wiki: Santa Barbara (Spanish: Santa Bárbara, meaning 'Saint Barbara') is a coastal city in Santa Barbara County, California, of which it is also the county seat.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: seaside city in Orange County, California, United States
description_wiki: Huntington Beach is a city in Orange County, California, United States.

description: county seat and city of Orange County, California, United States
description_wiki: Santa Ana (Spanish for 'Saint Anne') is a city in and the county seat of Orange County, California, United States.

description: city located in the South Bay region of Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Gardena is a city located in the South Bay region of Los Angeles County, California, United States.

description: beachfront neighborhood in Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: Venice is a neighborhood of the City of Los Angeles within the Westside region of Los Angeles County, California, United States.

description: Japanese American businessman
description_wiki: Kyutaro Abiko (安孫子 久太郎, Abiko Kyūtarō; 1865–1936) was a Japanese-born American businessman and newspaper editor.

description: unincorporated community in California
description_wiki: Cortez is an unincorporated community in Merced County, California.
